content.summaryText: Automated External Defibrillator Use in Cardiac Emergencies focuses on early recognition of ischemia or arrest and immediate protocol activation. Primary objective: Attach pads correctly.

content.questionText: In Automated External Defibrillator Use, which action best supports 'Attach pads correctly'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Assess circulation, start CPR when indicated, and prepare AED
Waiting for definitive tests before starting first-line emergency care
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: assess circulation, start CPR when indicated, and prepare AED. Practical emphasis: Follow voice prompts.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when persistent chest pain, hypotension, altered mental status, or pulselessness.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
